---
title: "Model Card: Home Credit Default Risk Model"
author: "Owen Simon"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Executive Summary

## Business Recommendation

(Write this last.)

This model estimates the probability that a loan applicant will default.  
At the selected decision threshold of **X%**, the model is expected to:

- Approve approximately X% of applicants  
- Reduce default losses by $X per 10,000 applications  
- Increase expected profit by $X compared to approving all applicants  

## Financial Impact

(Insert final business impact table here.)

## Key Caveats

- Model trained on historical data  
- No macroeconomic stress testing  
- Fairness gaps exist across demographic groups  

---

# 1. Model Details

## 1.1 Model Type and Version

- Model Type: Logistic Regression with L1 regularization (LASSO)  
- Implementation: `glmnet` via caret  
- Version: v1.0  
- Training Date: (insert date)

## 1.2 Training Data Summary

- Dataset: Home Credit Default Risk  
- Training observations: (insert count)  
- Features after preprocessing: (insert count)  
- Target variable: `TARGET`  
  - 1 = Default  
  - 0 = Repaid  

Class imbalance: X% default rate.

## 1.3 Key Hyperparameters

- Regularization type: L1  
- Optimal lambda: (insert)  
- Cross validation: 10-fold  
- Tuning metric: ROC AUC  

---

# 2. Intended Use

## 2.1 Intended Users

- Credit risk analysts  
- Underwriting teams  
- Lending strategy managers  

## 2.2 Decision Support

The model informs:

- Loan approval decisions  
- Risk-based pricing  
- Portfolio risk management  

## 2.3 Out of Scope

- Not a replacement for regulatory compliance checks  
- Not designed for small business or mortgage lending  
- Not validated for macroeconomic downturn scenarios  

---

# 3. Performance Metrics

## 3.1 Cross-Validated Performance

- CV AUC: `r cv_auc`  
- Precision at chosen threshold: (insert)  
- Recall at chosen threshold: (insert)

## 3.2 Kaggle Test Performance

- Kaggle AUC: (insert leaderboard score)

## 3.3 Threshold-Specific Performance

```{r}
# Placeholder objects to populate from modeling notebook
cv_auc <- NA
precision_at_thresh <- NA
recall_at_thresh <- NA
kaggle_auc <- NA

cv_auc
precision_at_thresh
recall_at_thresh
kaggle_auc
